tier: A3: APFS snapshot create on the certified crash-safe in-place COW checkpoint engine
host: Local WSL2 archlinux (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1) with a 256 MiB generated container passed through on SATA (disk0, bare APFS container; kernel synthesised /dev/disk1)
change: Implemented APFS snapshot CREATE as a true in-place crash-safe COW checkpoint commit (commitInPlaceSnapshotCreate + commitImageOnlySnapshotCreate / commitRawSnapshotCreate + CLI commit-image-snapshot-create / commit-raw-snapshot-create). Byte recipe harvested from a real macOS Sequoia sealed-system snapshot host-side (every previously-UNSURE field resolved against the live container). The commit freezes the live volume into a PHYSICAL superblock copy (o_oid == its own paddr, o_type OBJ_PHYSICAL|FS; keeps root_tree_oid, zeros omap/extentref/snap_meta), copies the extent-ref tree (frozen), emits a new omap-snapshot tree (subtype 0x13, fixed-kv, oms value all-zero) and snap-meta tree records (j_snap_metadata + j_snap_name), and re-points the volume omap header (om_snap_count / om_snapshot_tree_oid / om_most_recent_snap), volume superblock (num_snapshots, next_obj_id, apfs_fs_alloc_count) and container omap. The root fs-tree, the volume object-map tree, and the live extent-ref tree are left in place, shared with the snapshot. Fails closed on a volume that already carries a snapshot or a multi-level volume object map.
alloc_count_logic: apfs_fs_alloc_count is a LOGICAL count: a snapshot re-walks the catalog + frozen extent-ref + extents but not the live-only superblock / single-node object-map tree / single-node snap-meta tree, so the first snapshot grows the count to 2*before - 3. Decoupled from the physical spaceman delta (+3 new blocks). Verified against apfsck's walk (empty container 5 -> 7).
shared_fix: apfsck caught a latent shared bug: writeRotatedCib never stamped chunk 0's ci_xid, so the rotated cib violated 'cib xid == max chunk xid'. The A2 file paths masked it (their apfsck runs die earlier on a benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags' strictness). Fixed for every commit path; full unit suite stays green (no A2 regression).
linux_apfsck: apfsprogs apfsck on the generated snapshot is CLEAN: the snapshot is enumerated, the volume superblock block-count check (apfs_fs_alloc_count 7 == walked 7) passes, and the chunk-info xid check passes.
scenario: Format a 256 MiB container (volume SAKSNAP) -> commit-image-snapshot-create --snapshot-name sak.refactor.check (writer xid 2 -> 3) -> pass the bare-container image through to macOS recovery on SATA disk0.

apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_mount_rw: The macOS kernel auto-mounted the synthesised container /dev/disk1 read-write; fsck_apfs first reported 'container /dev/rdisk1 is mounted with write access'. fsck_apfs confirms 'The volume SAKSNAP was formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer and last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1)' - the Apple kernel parsed and modified our snapshot-bearing container.
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 3; the kernel mounted read-write and continued the checkpoint to transaction ID 5 with the snapshot (xid 3) intact.
apple_fsck_apfs.snapshot_checked: ** Checking the snapshot metadata tree. ** Checking the snapshot metadata. ** Checking snapshot 1 of 1 (sak.refactor.check, transaction ID 3) - Apple's checker enumerated our snapshot by name and xid and validated its metadata tree.
apple_fsck_apfs.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 with UUID 1298B31C-4EDE-4CAE-ACF1-78745F775C64 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.accounting_clean: 'Verifying volume object map space' and 'Verifying allocated space' both clean (no alloc_count mismatch, no overallocation).
apple_fsck_apfs.diskutil_cross_check: diskutil mount disk1s1 (Volume SAKSNAP mounted) then diskutil apfs listSnapshots disk1s1 reports 'Snapshot for disk1s1 (1 found)' -> Name: sak.refactor.check, XID: 3, Purgeable: Yes.
apple_fsck_apfs.evidence: fsck_apfs-snapshot-clean.png (snapshot enumerated + warning-free container/volume verdict); diskutil-listSnapshots.png (snapshot enumerated by name + xid); diskutil-list-physical.png (the 268 MB bare container on disk0)

note: Certifies A3 snapshot CREATE. Snapshot delete + revert are later A3 increments; the A3 matrix lane flips to Yes only when create/delete/revert are all certified. Unit test apfsWriter_inPlaceSnapshotCreateAddsSnapshot; full local suite green.
